jei.alone.waterskin.desc: Right-click a water source to fill it (4 draughts); hold right-click to drink. Raw water risks sickness — swamp/jungle water is worse. A skin can't go over a fire, so boil water clean in a fire-safe pot and pour it in, or fill from clean rain or a water cauldron.
jei.alone.flint_knife.desc: A cutting blade. Kill a hide-bearing animal while holding a blade (this, a sword, axe, or spear) to butcher the carcass for raw hide, bone, and sinew — a fists/pick kill wastes them. Hold right-click on grass/ferns/vines to strip 2-3 plant fibre at once (bare hands: 1-2).
jei.alone.tallow.desc: Rendered animal fat. Make it by cooking Raw Fat (from butchering big game) on a lit campfire or in a furnace. Craft Tallow + String to cast a candle for dim light.
jei.alone.pyrite.desc: A fire-starting stone that turns up now and then when you dig gravel. Craft Pyrite + a Flint Shard to make a Flint and Pyrite striker.
jei.alone.flint_and_pyrite.desc: A strike-a-light. Hold it and hold right-click on an UNLIT campfire to strike it alight — quick and near-effortless, but the spark needs dry tinder, so it fails in the rain (a bow drill also fails wet; only a forged ferro rod lights in the wet). Wears out after ~40 fires.
jei.alone.slingshot.desc: A ranged weapon for small game. Carry loose Rocks as ammo. Hold right-click to draw (a longer draw = more power and range), release to loose a pebble at what you're aiming at. Each shot spends one rock. Drops rabbits, birds, squirrels; barely dents big animals.
jei.alone.bug_net.desc: Right-click to wear it on your head. While worn, biting insects near standing water can't reach you — no stamina drain, no wetland fever. It occupies your HELMET slot (you can't also wear a helmet). A smudge fire (a lit campfire) does the same for anyone nearby.
jei.alone.wicker_shield.desc: A primitive shield. Hold right-click to raise it and block attacks like any shield. It has the lowest durability of the shields, and a large creature (bear, boar, ravager, golem) bashes it aside for LONGER than a sturdier wooden or metal shield.
